schedule.description: Story 1 — Red Bull wind tunnel crisis

stories.title: Verstappen Admits Red Bull in "Midfield Battle" — Crisis Deepens After Japan

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen after the Japanese GP: Red Bull is in "the midfield battle."

The FOUR-TIME world champion. 9th in the championship. 1.7 seconds per lap slower in China.

This is F1's biggest storyline of 2026. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Red Bull's 2026 problem? An aging wind tunnel that can't optimize the car for new regs.

Result: 16 points through 3 races. -60 from the championship leader.

Verstappen. Struggling. In the midfield. Let that sink in. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

article.headline: Verstappen Admits Red Bull Are in "The Midfield Battle" as Crisis Deepens After Japan

stories.title: FIA Technical Meeting Day 2 — April 20 Binding Vote in View

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The FIA's technical meeting continues today (Day 2). April 20 = binding vote.

On the table: raise super-clip from 250kW to 350kW. Cut energy recovery from 9MJ to 6MJ.

These changes could reshape the entire 2026 competitive order. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

article.headline: FIA's April 20 Vote Could Change the 2026 F1 Season as Technical Meetings Continue

stories.title: Antonelli's Historic Championship Lead — Is the Title Already His?

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli is 19 years and 216 days old.

He leads the F1 World Championship by 9 points.

He broke Lewis Hamilton's record as the youngest-ever championship leader.

The 2026 season might already belong to him. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

article.headline: Nineteen and Leading the World: Antonelli's Historic Championship Position Explained

stories.title: Miami GP Sprint Weekend Preview — Five-Week Wait Is Almost Over

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
F1 returns to action May 1-3 in Miami. And it's a SPRINT weekend.

Two race cancellations. A 5-week break. Every team scrambling to find pace.

Antonelli vs. Russell. Ferrari's upgrades. Verstappen's redemption arc.

Miami cannot come fast enough. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

article.headline: Five Weeks Away: What to Expect at the Miami GP Sprint Weekend on May 1-3

stories.title: Verstappen at Nurburgring — NLS3 Race Possible Saturday

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
While Red Bull fixes the RB22, Verstappen is racing a Mercedes-AMG GT3 at the Nurburgring.

NLS3 is tomorrow (April 11). He's also confirmed for qualifier races April 18-19 and the 24H on May 16.

The man just needs to race. Can't blame him. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

article.headline: Verstappen Takes to the Nurburgring as Red Bull Regroups During F1's Five-Week Break

stories.title: Ferrari's Monza Test April 22 — Everything Riding on Pre-Miami Upgrade

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Ferrari has confirmed a filming day at Monza on April 22.

On the agenda: new floor, ADUO power unit software upgrade, revised rear wing.

Hamilton and Leclerc will share the wheel. They're coming for Mercedes in Miami. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: 2026 F1 Constructors' Battle — Mercedes Lead, Ferrari/McLaren Fight for Second

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
2026 F1 Constructors after 3 races:

Mercedes: 135 pts
Ferrari: 90 pts
McLaren: 46 pts
Red Bull: 16 pts

This is what a regulation reset looks like. 2026 is already wild. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

## Sources Used
- F1.com: Official standings, race reports, regulation articles, calendar
- ESPN: Race reports, Verstappen crisis analysis
- Sky Sports: Red Bull shortcomings, Bearman/regulations
- Planet F1: Verstappen wind tunnel analysis
- GPFans: FIA meeting timeline, NLS3 reference, next race schedule
- RacingNews365: Standings, Miami dates, Nurburgring
- The Race: FIA proposals, Ferrari test
- Scuderia Fans: Ferrari Monza/Mugello/Fiorano test details
- Silverstone.co.uk: Antonelli youngest leader context
- Motorsport.com: Japan GP results
